title: Xtra Shaggy Mexican Poppy Horchata
description: A unique, briny twist on traditional horchata featuring ground poppy seeds and a wood-fired bake. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a blender, combine the ground poppy seeds, evaporated milk, water, salt, smoked paprika, and garlic powder. Blend until smooth.
Strain the mixture into a large bowl to remove any pulp or sediment.
Add the chopped cilantro and lime juice to the bowl and stir to combine.
Pour the mixture into 4-6 small baking dishes or ramekins.
Bake in the wood-fired oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the tops are lightly browned and set.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve warm, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days.
To serve, stir the horchata well and pour into glasses or cups.
